classes.means: Fixes an irreducible premise, entity model, or identity-bearing assumption the rest of the stack depends on.
classes.does_not_mean: Not a full protocol, procedure, or mature implementation standard.
classes.reading_boundary: Read as a load-bearing premise and do not infer downstream procedures the text does not state.

classes.means: Fixes a structural relation, hierarchy, or stack-wide design rule that routes interpretation across repos.
classes.does_not_mean: Not every downstream clause, witness rule, or package detail is settled merely because the architecture is load-bearing.
classes.reading_boundary: Read as stack structure and relation logic, not as complete doctrine.

classes.means: Defines bounded execution, recovery, or handling logic for real operation under specified conditions.
classes.does_not_mean: Not a universal law, final ontology, or blanket permission claim.
classes.reading_boundary: Read as operational discipline within scope, not as unlimited theory or mature normative closure.

classes.means: Offers a serious normative proposal that may guide review and citation while remaining explicitly non-final.
classes.does_not_mean: Not stable mature core, not settled law of the corpus, and not immune from revision.
classes.reading_boundary: Read as strong draft material with explicit revision risk.

classes.means: Records a protocol, pattern, or stability claim grounded in observed or practice-derived regularity.
classes.does_not_mean: Not a universal law and not a guarantee that every context behaves the same way.
classes.reading_boundary: Read as evidence of recurrent behavior, not as total doctrine.

classes.means: Shows that a constrained operational form can be built, closed, and inspected in executable reality.
classes.does_not_mean: Not productization, not market readiness, and not a replacement for normative proof.
classes.reading_boundary: Read as feasibility evidence under constraints, not as finished platform doctrine.

classes.means: Defines what can be witnessed, checked, replayed, challenged, or verified at the evidence layer.
classes.does_not_mean: Not a full ontological settlement and not automatic proof of every interpretation placed around it.
classes.reading_boundary: Read as a claim about reviewability and admissibility, not about every broader conclusion.

classes.means: Carries recognition, perception, and human-facing interpretive force that helps readers see what narrower protocol layers alone may not surface.
classes.does_not_mean: Not normative proof, not protocol evidence, and not doctrinal replacement.
classes.reading_boundary: Read as companion illumination only; it can clarify recognition without replacing proof.

classes.means: Marks territory that is intentionally bounded, incomplete, or held open rather than closed into doctrine.
classes.does_not_mean: Not hidden completed doctrine, not weak mature norm, and not silent permission to fill gaps by inference.
classes.reading_boundary: Read as deliberate non-closure; the safe reading is restraint rather than completion by guesswork.

classes.means: Constrains entry, citation, routing, sync, terminology, acceptance, or other corpus-control behavior.
classes.does_not_mean: Not primary doctrine and not a substitute for the artifact whose meaning it routes.
classes.reading_boundary: Read as reading-control and maintenance discipline, not as the underlying claim source itself.

examples.artifact: c = a + b / AGI core framing
Cold Wake / Continuity Bundle recovery logic
examples.assertion_class: operational rule / execution discipline or evidence-layer / witness-verification claim, depending on whether the question is handling or admissibility
draft normative proposal unless a narrower canonical surface explicitly gives them stronger force

examples.artifact: primer / stack lock / sync / supersession / terminology / acceptance layers

fail_closed_rule: If the claim force of a text is unclear, read it at the weaker compatible class until a more canonical surface clarifies otherwise.
